code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Relative clause templates (subject omitted)
    rel_templates = {
        "league": "plays in the {o} league",
        "manager": "is managed by {o}",
        "position": "holds the position of {o}",
        "ground": "has ground {o}",
        "birthDate": "was born on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {o}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {o}",
        "club": "plays for {o}",
        "draftTeam": "was drafted by {o}",
        "height": "is {o} meters tall",
        "youthclub": "started at youth club {o}",
        "currentclub": "currently plays for {o}",
        "weight": "weighs {o} kilograms",
        "deathPlace": "died in {o}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started his active years in {o}",
        "currentteam": "currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": "made his debut with {o}",
        "draftPick": "was pick number {o}",
        "draftRound": "was selected in round {o}",
        "formerTeam": "formerly played for {o}",
        "playerNumber": "has player number {o}",
        "college": "attended {o}",
        "draftYear": "was drafted in {o}",
        "coach": "is coached by {o}",
        "owner": "is owned by {o}",
        "city": "is based in {o}",
        "leader": "is led by {o}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {o}",
        "language": "has language {o}",
        "generalManager": "has general manager {o}",
        "chairman": "has chairman {o}",
        "season": "participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": "has currency {o}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {o}",
        "stadium": "has stadium {o}",
        "universityTeam": "is the university team of {o}",
        "mostChampions": "has most championships {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {o}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {o}",
        "country": "is in {o}",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset {o}",
        "timeZone": "has time zone {o}",
        "areaTotal": "has total area {o}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Determine the primary subject:
    # Prefer a subject that never appears as an object in any triple.
    subjects = [t.subject for t in triples]
    objects = {t.object for t in triples}
    primary_candidates = [s for s in subjects if s not in objects]
    if primary_candidates:
        main_subject = primary_candidates[0]
    else:
        # fallback to the most frequent subject
        from collections import Counter
        main_subject = Counter(subjects).most_common(1)[0][0]
    clauses = []

    for t in triples:
        # Clean object for certain predicates (e.g., remove parentheses in weight)
        obj = t.object
        if t.predicate == "weight":
            # Remove anything in parentheses and strip whitespace
            import re
            obj = re.sub(r"\s*\(.*?\)", "", obj).strip()
        tmpl = rel_templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(o=obj)
        else:
            # Generic fallback preserving predicate order
            clause = f"{t.predicate} {t.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Build sentence: main subject + first clause, then join remaining with commas and a final "and"
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = f"{main_subject} {clauses[0]}."
    else:
        first = clauses[0]
        rest = clauses[1:]
        if len(rest) == 1:
            sentence = f"{main_subject} {first}, and {rest[0]}."
        else:
            middle = ", ".join(rest[:-1])
            sentence = f"{main_subject} {first}, {middle}, and {rest[-1]}."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
